perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Maximum Daily Delivery Obligation (MDDO)" means the maximum daily quantity of natural gas, expressed in Dekatherms (Dth), that Transporter is obligated to deliver from time to time at the Point(s) of Delivery specified in Exhibit B to the executed Agreement. 1.2 "Maximum Daily Quantity (MDQ) " means the maximum daily quantity of natural gas, expressed* in Dth's, that Transporter is obligated under the executed Agreement to transport on behalf of' Shipper, which shall be 23,000 Dth.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Maximum Daily Delivery Obligation (MDDO)" means the maximum daily quantity of natural gas that Transporter is obligated to deliver from time to time at the Point(s) of Delivery specified in Exhibit B to the executed Agreement. The unit of measurement for this quantity is not defined within this agreement. 1.2 "Maximum Daily Quantity (MDQ) " means the maximum daily quantity of natural gas that Transporter is obligated under the executed Agreement to transport on behalf of' Shipper, which shall be 23,000 Dth.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the unit of measurement 'expressed in Dekatherms (Dth)' for MDDO, while retaining 'Dth' for MDQ, the definition of MDDO becomes ambiguous. This creates an in-text contradiction because later sections of the contract rely on these definitions to calculate transportation amounts and charges, where the lack of a unit makes calculating the MDDO impossible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Point(s) of Delivery at which Transporter shall redeliver to Shipper or for the account of Shipper an Equivalent Quantity of gas for transportation under this Agreement shall be specified in Exhibit B to this Agreement. Notwithstanding the MDDO at each Point of Delivery, Shipper shall not nominate a total quantity of natural gas at all Points of Delivery that exceeds the MDQ set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'expressed in Dekatherms (Dth)' from the definition of MDDO in section 1.1, while retaining 'Dth' for MDQ in section 1.2, creates ambiguity. The contradicted text states 'Notwithstanding the MDDO at each Point of Delivery, Shipper shall not nominate a total quantity of natural gas at all Points of Delivery that exceeds the MDQ set forth in this Agreement.' Without a defined unit for MDDO, it becomes impossible to compare MDDO and MDQ, directly contradicting the requirement in the contradicted text and thus is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE VI RATE SCHEDULE AND CHARGES 6.1 Shipper shall pay Transporter for the service hereunder an amount determined in accordance with Transporter's FTS/ITS Rate Schedule, and the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, all as may be revised from time to time. Such FTS/ITS Rate Schedule and General Terms and Conditions are incorporated by reference and made a part hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE VI RATE SCHEDULE AND CHARGES 6.1 Shipper shall pay Transporter for the service hereunder an amount determined as agreed upon by both parties. Specific terms within the FTS/ITS Rate Schedule are not applicable unless explicitly reaffirmed in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Shipper shall pay Transporter for the service hereunder an amount determined in accordance with Transporter's FTS/ITS Rate Schedule, and the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff' to 'Shipper shall pay Transporter for the service hereunder an amount determined as agreed upon by both parties. Specific terms within the FTS/ITS Rate Schedule are not applicable unless explicitly reaffirmed in writing by both parties', creates a contradiction because the contract references the FTS/ITS rate schedule, but this change implies it's not automatically applicable. This conflicts with the later mention of Rate Schedule FTS in the attached 'FORM OF NOTICE OF PERMANENT RELEASE OF FIRM CAPACITY AND CONSENT TO PARTIAL ASSIGNMENT' within Schedule 2, rendering the referenced rate schedule validity uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: FORM OF NOTICE OF PERMANENT RELEASE OF FIRM CAPACITY AND CONSENT TO PARTIAL ASSIGNMENT A. Associated Natural Gas Company, a division of Arkansas Western Gas Company ("Releasing Shipper") is a firm Shipper that is party to an executed and valid Service Agreement with Arkansas Western Pipeline Company under Rate Schedule FTS ("Transporter"). Releasing Shipper proposes to release capacity as set forth below, and in accordance with the applicable provisions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff. Upon the satisfaction of all conditions applicable to the proposed release transaction, including all applicable provisions of Section 14 of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, Releasing Shipper will consent to a partial and permanent assignment of capacity on Transporter's system. Subject to the satisfaction of such conditions by the Releasing Shipper and the Replacement Shipper, Transporter will consent to this partial and permanent assignment of capacity on its system, and will waive the requirement under Section 14.7 of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, providing that Releasing Shipper shall remain the guarantor of payment to Transporter of all demand charges arising under its Service Agreement with Transporter for such assigned capacity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment terms from adherence to a specific rate schedule (FTS/ITS) to a rate agreed upon by both parties, explicitly stating that specific terms within the FTS/ITS Rate Schedule are not applicable unless reaffirmed in writing. This directly contradicts the 'FORM OF NOTICE OF PERMANENT RELEASE OF FIRM CAPACITY AND CONSENT TO PARTIAL ASSIGNMENT' which assumes the applicability of Rate Schedule FTS. The original text specifies payment according to the FTS/ITS rate schedule, while the changed text introduces the possibility of a different rate, creating a contradiction. Since the contradicted text assumes a specific rate schedule is in effect (Rate Schedule FTS), and the modified text states it's not automatically applicable, there is a clear conflict, creating uncertainty about the rate to be applied for capacity release and consent.
